title: Pellionia biakense
taxonomy: Pellionia biakense is classified in the family Urticaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the nettle family. This family consists of approximately 2,000 species, spread across 98 genera. Pellionia biakense is native to New Guinea. It belongs to the genus Pellionia, which is a group of evergreen, climbing or twining plants. The classification of Pellionia biakense highlights its unique characteristics and its position within the larger plant kingdom.

intro: Pellionia biakense is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family, and is native to the island of Biak in Papua, Indonesia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and infections, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Pellionia biakense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pellionia biakense is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
